title: test: remove low-value smoke/constant tests
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Remove low/zero-value tests surfaced by the test-suite audit (no real coverage lost):

- Delete `fixtures.test.ts` (a fixture smoke test with zero assertions), `httpError.test.ts` (no-logic constructor — covered via real code in `fetchUpstream.test.ts`), and `openai-schema.test.ts` (only exercises Zod `.passthrough()` on fabricated keys)
- Delete the duplicate `fetchUpstream + HttpError integration` block (folds the `instanceof HttpError` guarantee into the existing test)
- Drop two constant-equals-itself lines (`POLLEN_BILLING_PRECISION`, `CONSENT_PERMISSIONS`) — for the latter, route `keys` through the real `sanitize()` so the allowlist stays pinned through actual filtering
- Trim a hardcoded-hex theme assertion and the per-attribute icon render smoke-test

Net −69 lines, 8 files. biome clean; all 5 affected suites pass with no new failures. Keep-but-note tests (vcr, r2-storage, byop, wallet, Dialog) untouched.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Sweeping up the test suite
gist.blurb: We've swept away some low-value smoke tests and redundant assertions to keep our test suite fast and focused. Less clutter, same great coverage!
gist.summary: Removed 69 lines of low-value and redundant tests across the enter, gen, and UI packages, including zero-assertion smoke tests and constant-equals-itself checks.
gist.impact: Developers will experience a slightly cleaner test suite without any loss in actual code coverage.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Nomnom creature cleaning up a retro server room. Polly sweeping dust bunnies, Nomnom eating old test papers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping up small pixelated dust bunnies in a retro server room. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of old test papers. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
